bridge_resolution.truth_boundary: Published split rosters are now materialized and exact sequence groups are resolved. Some historical identifiers may remain accession-ambiguous, so candidate UniRef overlap is reported separately from exact accession overlap.

supplemental_evidence.summary: Released D-SCRIPT pair files were recovered locally, including human train/test and a fly transfer cohort.
details: `human_train.tsv` contains 421,792 labeled pairs spanning 15,816 unique Ensembl proteins.
`human_test.tsv` contains 52,725 labeled pairs spanning 15,525 unique Ensembl proteins.
`fly_test.tsv` contains 55,000 labeled pairs spanning 19,213 FlyBase proteins.
The released identifiers are Ensembl/FlyBase protein IDs rather than warehouse-native `protein_ref` or UniProt accessions.

supplemental_evidence.summary: The Struct2Graph repository exposes the split-building code and it is example-level randomization rather than accession-grouped separation.
details: `interactions_data.txt` contains 10,004 labeled PDB-pair examples and `list_of_prots.txt` contains 4,031 accession-to-PDB-chain rows.
The released `create_examples.py` shuffles merged examples three times and then slices them into 80% train, 10% test, and 10% dev.
That repo logic operates on pair examples, not on proteins, accession roots, UniRef groups, or source-separated components.
